regions.text: Our bodies control themselves in many other ways. For example, the temperature of our bodies (and those of all warm-blooded animals) is controlled; our blood temperature is kept at 98.6 degrees Fahrenheit, or near it.
BOILING: 212 F / 100 C. BODY: 98.6 F / 37 C. ICE: 32 F / 0 C. -40 F / -40 C.
When a person is very ill (sick) some of the automatic controls of his body stop working. His temperature, measured by a Fahrenheit thermometer, may go as high as 103°F or 104°F.
temperature: measure of heat. warm: middle degree of heat. degree: unit of temperature. ill: sick. thermometer: instrument for measuring heat.
